case_name: The Defendant says there were ten months to trial when the application was made and that there is little or no prejudice to the Claimant if the admission is withdrawn. Although the Claimant will have to provide a witness statement (as will his wife) as to the facts and go back to July 2015 to do so, a witness statement as to the facts must have been taken before proceedings were commenced and that can be used now. It is said the Claimant can give evidence about the consultation on the 20thJuly 2015 and what he said about the duration of the neck lump. Further the Claimant would have obtained expert evidence on breach of duty prior to issue of the claim given the principles expressed inPantelli Associates Limited v Corporate City Developments[2010] EWHC 3189 (TCC)and therefore an expert is available to give the necessary evidence. Therefore the issues can be resolved in court. The trial need not be vacated it is said and a strict timetable will permit the case to go ahead in October 2021.

summary.brief: The Defendant says there were ten months to trial when the application was made and that there is little or no prejudice to the Claimant if the admission is withdrawn. Although the Claimant will have to provide a witness statement (as will his wife) as to the facts and go back to July 2015 to do so, a witness statement as to the facts must have been taken before proceedings were commenced and that can be used now. It is said the Claimant can give evidence about the consultation on the 20thJuly 2

evidence_index.text: The Claimant's claim arises out of consultations he underwent with the Defendant on four occasions, namely 20th July 2015, 17th August 2015, 8th September 2015 and 29th September 2015.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Throughout the period in question (July to September 2015) when the Defendant saw the Claimant the neck lump grew in size. The lump later transpired to be a squamous cell carcinoma requiring surgery.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr Reece FRCS an ENT Consultant relied upon by the Defendant in his report of 15 th January 2021 stated at Paragraph 4.03 " if the Claimant had been referred on the 20 th July 2015 and thereafter progression to surgery at the same rate that actually occurred the Claimant would have been operated on 3 rd September 2015".
evidence_index.speaker: Mr Reece, ENT Consultant

evidence_index.text: Liability was admitted in the defence dated 26 th March 2020 (served on 1 st April 2020) in relation to the first appointment on the 20 th July 2015 in that the Defendant failed to refer the Claimant in accordance with the NICE guidelines.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Nurse Wiltshire concluded that the Defendant was negligent on the 20 th July 2015 in a number of respects including failing to refer the Claimant in accordance with the NICE guidelines.
evidence_index.speaker: Nurse Wiltshire

evidence_index.text: The greater prejudice in my judgement would fall upon the Claimant if I were to allow the application.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: In my judgement the point raised lacks any real prospect of success.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: I refuse to allow the Defendant to make the amendment sought.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Sore throat, dry mouth, and neck lump
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient reports a neck lump present since before his birthday on 9th July 2015, with a sore throat and dry mouth.

focused_exam.neck: 4cm cyst-like lump under the right mandibular

requestables.name: ENT Specialist Consultation
requestables.description: Consultation with ENT specialist for further evaluation of neck lump

reveal.clinical_significance: Delayed referral led to larger tumor size
reveal.decision_impact: Earlier referral could have led to less invasive surgery

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to the health center with symptoms

timeline_phases.name: Delayed Referral
timeline_phases.description: Period during which referral was delayed

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient presents with neck lump and sore throat
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Refer to ENT specialist under 2-week wait pathway
options.clinical_reasoning: NICE guidelines recommend urgent referral for neck lumps

options.description: Advise patient to suck lemons and monitor
options.clinical_reasoning: Assumed diagnosis of Sialolithiasis without further investigation

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant advised patient to suck lemons
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed it was a blocked saliva duct

expected_action_court.description: Referral to ENT specialist should have been made

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to follow NICE guidelines for neck lumps

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent more extensive surgery due to delayed referral
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Loss of function to right shoulder and arm, Horner's Syndrome

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to refer under 2-week wait pathway
Causation established - delay led to more invasive surgery

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to refer under 2-week wait pathway
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Earlier referral would have led to less invasive surgery

allegations.allegation: Failure to refer under 2-week wait pathway
